perturbation.original_text: d) Payment terms: For each Kallo Mobile care suite of products license ordered by Agent from Kallo, Agent shall pay to Kallo the transfer price set forth in Schedule A. 50% of the total amount to be paid with the signed purchase order, 35% upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port and 15% upon completion of the installation. Upon delivery of the system the Agent is responsible for getting the signed EULA from the customer before the installation process starts.
perturbation.changed_text: d) Payment terms: For each Kallo Mobile care suite of products license ordered by Agent from Kallo, Agent shall pay to Kallo the transfer price set forth in Schedule A. 30% of the total amount to be paid with the signed purchase order, 55% upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port and 15% upon completion of the installation. Upon delivery of the system the Agent is responsible for getting the signed EULA from the customer before the installation process starts.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the payment terms. The original text specifies a 50% initial payment, 35% upon shipping and 15% upon completion of installation. The altered text now specifies a 30% initial payment, 55% upon shipping and 15% upon completion of installation. This directly contradicts the original payment percentages, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over which payment schedule should be followed, specifically the initial percentage on total amount and the payment upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port.
perturbation.contradicted_text: d) Payment terms: For each Kallo Mobile care suite of products license ordered by Agent from Kallo, Agent shall pay to Kallo the transfer price set forth in Schedule A. 30% of the total amount to be paid with the signed purchase order, 55% upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port and 15% upon completion of the installation. Upon delivery of the system the Agent is responsible for getting the signed EULA from the customer before the installation process starts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment percentages from 50%, 35%, and 15% to 30%, 55%, and 15%. The contradicted text states the payment terms are 30%, 55%, and 15%. This creates a contradiction, as the original text specified a different payment schedule, directly conflicting with the contradicted text which now agrees with the changed text. This inconsistency directly creates an in-text contradiction because it alters the initial payment and shipping percentages, making it unclear which payment schedule the parties should adhere to, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: a) Term: This Agreement shall enter into effect on the date it is signed by both parties as shown below.
perturbation.changed_text: a) Term: This Agreement shall enter into effect 30 days after the date it is signed by both parties as shown below.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the effective date of the agreement. The original text states the agreement begins on the date of signing, while the modified text delays the effective date by 30 days. This contradiction affects the overall timeline of the agreement and contradicts any sections that depend on the original effective date. It particularly contradicts those milestones which were expected to be achieved upon singing of the contract, such as sales targets, certification, exclusivity of territory, etc.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) Certified Sales Agent: Agent accepts and acknowledges that the Kallo 3 day Sales and 5 day Technical Training Program is required to be completed by the Agent within 90 days of execution of this agreement. Agent is required to pay a Training program fee of $10,000.00 for the Kallo Mobile care suite of Products on or before the date of execution of this agreement for the Authorization of Agent under this agreement by Kallo. Agent shall pay $1000/- per day for any additional days of training requested by Agent and all travel expenses plus per diem for the Kallo representative instructing the program will be due and payable to Kallo on or before execution of this agreement. Certification is mandatory for Agent to become certified to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products licensed under this agreement and to benefit from commissions allocated to Agent as outlined in Schedule A of this agreement. Non-compliance of this Section (2), subparagraph (c) on the part of the Agent will automatically void this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the effective date of the agreement to be 30 days after signing, contradicting the contradicted text. The contradicted text requires the agent to complete training and pay fees 'on or before the date of execution of this agreement.' Since the execution date (signing date) is now different from the effective date, it creates a contradiction regarding when the training and fees are due, potentially voiding the agreement based on non-compliance, as stated in the last sentence of the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections provide conflicting deadlines or obligations.
